summary: The Lipoarabinomannan (LAM) assay in urine is primarily used for detecting active Mycobacterium tuberculosis infections. This test is especially valuable in identifying pulmonary or extrapulmonary TB in immunocompromised individuals due to its sensitivity in diagnosing latent-to-active disease progression.
